channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Federal judge dismisses Trump administration lawsuit against Boston ICE cooperation limits
item.description: A federal judge has dismissed a lawsuit brought by the Trump administration that sought to block a Boston ordinance limiting local cooperation with U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE). The ...

item.title: Five persons, including two children, die in Abeokuta–Lagos expressway bus crash
item.description: No fewer than five persons, among them two children, lost their lives on Thursday in a road accident that happened at Iyana Egbado along the Abeokuta–Lagos Expressway in Ogun State. The crash involved...

item.title: Siha District to host West Kilimanjaro Forest Challenge attracting over 800 participants
item.description: Siha District in Tanzania is preparing to host a major sports and tourism event known as the West Kili Forest Tour Challenge, scheduled for June 20–21, 2026. The event is expected to bring together mo...

item.title: Labor’s proposed capital gains tax changes spark concern among start-ups and young investors
item.description: The article examines the political and economic debate surrounding the Australian Labor government’s proposed overhaul of capital gains tax (CGT). The reforms are intended to reduce generational wealt...

item.title: Tropical Storm Domeng enters Philippine Area of Responsibility, may intensify but no landfall expected
item.description: Tropical Storm Domeng (international name Jangmi) officially entered the Philippine Area of Responsibility (PAR) early Friday, May 29, 2026, becoming the country’s fourth tropical cyclone of the year ...

item.title: Firefighters respond to significant residential fire in Manotick
item.description: Ottawa Fire Services responded to a major house fire in the Manotick neighbourhood on May 28, 2026. Emergency crews arrived promptly after receiving multiple calls about the blaze. Firefighters worked...

item.title: Rising Bow River levels cause flooding in Banff region
item.description: Heavy rainfall and snowmelt have caused the Bow River in Alberta to rise significantly, leading to flooding in and around the Banff area. Local authorities are monitoring river levels closely, issuing...

item.title: FireLake Grill House in Umhlanga: Decent Ambience but Mixed Food Experience
item.description: Food writer Frank Chemaly and Ingrid Shevlin visited FireLake Grill House at the Radisson Blu Hotel in Umhlanga to assess its offerings. The restaurant impresses with modern décor, floor-to-ceiling gl...

item.title: TASAF and HESLB conduct financial literacy training for loan applicants to improve loan management
item.description: Tanzania Social Action Fund (TASAF) and the Higher Education Students’ Loans Board (HESLB) have carried out a joint financial education initiative aimed at improving understanding among loan applicant...

item.title: Government launches urban renewal programme covering 35 towns to improve infrastructure and housing
item.description: Tanzania has rolled out a major urban renewal programme targeting 35 towns and cities across the country in one of the most ambitious infrastructure development efforts in recent years. The initiative...

item.title: DOE sees possible reduction in fuel prices next week
item.description: The Department of Energy (DOE) has indicated that there is a strong possibility of an oil price rollback in the Philippines next week, bringing potential relief to consumers who have been dealing with...

item.title: Cambodia Orders Deportation of African Residents Amid Immigration Concerns
item.description: Cambodia imeamua kutoa amri ya kuhamisha wote raia wa Afrika wanaoishi nchini humo, hatua ambayo imeibua wasiwasi kutoka kwa mashirika ya haki za binadamu na wataalamu wa kimataifa. Serikali ya Phnom ...

item.title: Taxi boss Joe Sibanyoni and co-accused granted bail in R2m extortion case
item.description: Mpumalanga taxi boss Joe “Ferrari” Sibanyoni, along with his three co-accused Bafana Sindane, Mvimbi Masilela and Philemon Msiza, have been released on bail of R70,000 each after the state chose not t...

item.title: Forensic expert urges proper risk assessment in Pretoria Boys High school threat case
item.description: A leading forensic psychologist has cautioned that remorse alone should not be used to determine whether a Pretoria Boys High School learner accused of making violent threats poses a future risk. The ...

item.title: U.S. and Iran Agree in Principle to Extend Ceasefire and Continue Nuclear Talks
item.description: The United States and Iran have reportedly reached a tentative agreement to extend their fragile ceasefire by 60 days while continuing negotiations over Tehran’s nuclear program. According to a U.S. s...

item.title: eThekwini CFO Clarifies Wife’s Role and Employment Process
item.description: Dr Sandile Mnguni, CFO of eThekwini Municipality, has publicly addressed concerns regarding his wife Slindile’s employment at the municipality and her role on the Bid Evaluation Committee (BEC). He em...

item.title: Edward overhears Jacqueline’s treatment of Katherine in Episode 52 of The Alibi
item.description: Episode 52 of the ABS-CBN television drama series The Alibi focuses on rising tension between key characters Edward, Jacqueline, and Katherine. In this episode, Edward accidentally overhears a reveali...

item.title: Review of Sam Campbell’s mockumentary series Make That Movie and its surreal comedic style
item.description: The Guardian reviews Sam Campbell’s new Channel 4 sitcom Make That Movie, describing it as an absurd, high-concept mockumentary that may be one of the most original comedy series of the year. Known fo...

item.title: Supreme Court directs nationwide integration of all emergency helplines into single 112 number within three months
item.description: The Supreme Court of India has issued a significant directive mandating that all States and Union Territories integrate their existing emergency and ambulance helplines into a single unified emergency...

item.title: How transaction history and business activity data are improving SME loan access
item.description: Financial technology is changing how small and medium enterprises (SMEs) in emerging markets access loans, with transaction history and business activity now playing a key role in credit assessment. T...
